Picture windows, also known as fixed windows, do not open or close. The non-operable design enhances views, energy efficiency and weatherability. Fixed windows are versatile and can be used alone or in combination with other window styles to create an eye-catching focal point that maximizes natural light.

Picture windows are stationary with no moving parts and come in a wide range of sizes to accommodate your home’s needs.

  • Use large picture windows to brighten your home and showcase your outside view.
  • Illuminate hard-to-reach areas with a wide selection o f horizontal and vertical sizes.
  • Combine with other window styles to create a dramatic focal point.
  • Ideal for vaulted ceilings or dormers where operable windows are not necessary.
  • Non-operating design enhances efficiency and weather resistance.

Picture windows are one of the most energy – efficient window styles since they do not open or close and can be configured to meet ENERGY STAR® guidelines for any area of the country. The stationary design offers increased protection against air and water infiltration. They are made of premium vinyl that insulates your home from outside conditions for year – round comfort.

Bay and bow windows are created by combining three or more windows at an angle. They arch outward and extend beyond your home’s exterior wall. Their unique style and dimension provide a home with more natural light, open views, and interior space.

  • Bay & bow windows add dimension and architectural character to both inside and outside of your home. The top benefits of bay and bow windows include:
  • Enhance curb appeal with the unique dimension of a bay and bow window.
  • Increase interior space with a window seat that creates a cozy reading area or breakfast nook.
  • Illuminate with windows that extend outward and direct more light into your home.
  • Provides enhanced views of the outside.

The key differences between bay and bow windows are the angles and number of windows.

Bay windows are available with a 30-degree angle or a 45-degree angle, which creates a more dramatic appearance and dimension to any room. Bay windows feature either double hung or casement windows on each side of a center picture window.

Bow windows , available in a 3-, 4- or 5-lite design, always have a 10-degree angle, which creates a more rounded, circular look. Bow windows feature equal-sized double hung, casement and picture windows.

Bay and bow windows have a vintage feel and are suited for almost any traditional American home including colonials, cape cods, Victorians, ranch homes, farmhouses or cottages. With the right customizations, a bay or bow window can easily be incorporated into contemporary home designs.

What are the advantages of casement windows?

The main advantages of casement windows come from their hinged design that offers unobstructed views, maximum ventilation and natural light. Other benefits include: - Multi-point locking system tightly seals sash for excellent weather resistance and energy efficiency. - Convenient access to open and close from hard-to-reach places. - Easy open nesting crank handle that won’t interfere with window treatments. - Durable stainless-steel operating system. - Interior screen is easily removed from inside the home.

Simonton custom-built Casement windows are made to your exact specifications for accurate installation. The cost of replacement casement windows depends on different factors including the size, colors, hardware, grilles and energy-efficient customizations. Double hung windows are a popular choice for any style of home. They feature a top and bottom sash that slide up and down to provide two levels of ventilation. The inward tilting sash makes them easy to clean making Double Hung windows ideal for multi-level homes.

What are the advantages of double hung windows?

Double hung windows offer timeless style with two operating sash that slide up and down. Additional benefits include: - Both sash tilt inward for easy in-home cleaning of the interior and exterior sash, making double hung windows an excellent choice for multi-story homes. - Offers enhanced airflow and cross ventilation using top and bottom sash. Open the top sash only for safer ventilation with children and pets. - Space-saving by design, double hung windows open flush in the frame and won’t impede exterior walkways or patios.

How can I customize my double hung windows?

Simonton Double Hung windows are custom-built to your home’s specifications for precise installation. Specialty windows denote unique shapes and operating styles available that can add beauty and character to your home. Combine them with standard windows or feature them alone and to make a visual statement. See the options below.

Radius Windows

You can choose to have your specialty window operable to let in fresh air or fixed to only let in light, the choice is yours.

In an arch-top or radius window, the bottom half of the window is rectangular while the top is an arch or half-circle adding architectural interest to a room.

Symmetrical circle windows can act as a focal point in bathrooms, entryways, stairways, and closets while providing natural light.

Bay & Bow Windows

Add more space to your home with a shelf for decorative elements or even a permanent window seat which is commonly used in living rooms or dining rooms.

A bay window is typically a combination of three windows of varying widths that dramatically extend from your home.

A bow window is very similar to a bay window, except it's generally composed of four or more windows joined at equal angles to form a more even curve.

Garden Windows

Because they extend beyond the house, place your garden window where it won't interfere with sidewalks, patios, or other exterior features.

Garden windows contain an interior shelf for plants and herbs making it the perfect solution for avid chefs and gardeners.

Because garden windows offer side vents, you can enjoy natural light as well as ventilation in your kitchen.

Perfect for framing amazing views, picture windows are fixed windows that do not open.

When to Choose Picture Windows?

Picture windows are fixed windows that do not open, offering a seamless connection to nature without the distraction of frames or sashes. They’re ideal for homeowners looking for replacement picture windows and contractors in search of windows.

With large picture windows, your home will bask in abundant natural light, minimizing the need for artificial lighting and creating a warm, inviting atmosphere. Many homeowners choose them for living rooms, dining rooms, entryways, and office spaces.

Picture windows are also a popular choice when you want floor-to-ceiling windows, such as on an enclosed patio. Or, place them at the back of your home in bedrooms and offices so you can keep an eye on the kids as they play.

Picture windows not only elevate your home’s visual appeal but also its energy efficiency. Because they’re sealed, they can help maintain consistent indoor temperatures, reducing energy costs and promoting a more comfortable living environment.

Embrace the outdoors with awning windows, a popular choice that offers ventilation and light in small areas while protecting the inside of your home from the elements.

When Do You Need Awning Windows?

Awning windows hinge at the top and open outward, creating a “roof” over the window that allows fresh air in while keeping rain and snow out. They effortlessly open and close with a crank handle and are typically wider than they are tall, giving them some unique benefits.

Awning windows provide better airflow than many other styles. Plus, small awning window sizes mean they can fit into tight spaces. They’re perfect for areas that need good ventilation but where larger windows aren’t practical, like basements, laundry rooms, and bathrooms.

Awning-style windows are also a good choice when you want to place a window high up on a wall, due to design considerations or for privacy. They can be stacked on top of each other, combined with other window styles, or placed around nonopening windows.

With options for vinyl, fiberglass, and aluminum awning windows, you can find a style that suits your home. Aluminum frames are strong and sleek, while vinyl awning windows are popular for their insulation properties and low maintenance.

Invite light, air, and ambiance into your home with casement windows. Ideal for any room, our casement windows are available for both new construction and replacement projects.

What are the Benefits of Casement Windows?

Casement windows are hinged at the right or left side and open outward like a door. They provide clear, expansive views and excellent ventilation for any room in your home.

Casement windows maximize natural light, fresh air flow, and views of the outdoors with a design that opens fully outward. Offering easy operation with a crank mechanism, they’re also ideal for hard-to-reach places.

Casement windows are customizable with a variety of colors, finishes, and materials, including vinyl, wood, and aluminum. You can also choose which side opens, or go with double casement windows for even more airflow.

Enhanced energy efficiency comes standard thanks to the tight seal of casement windows. Our vinyl options are heat, water, and UV resistant, offering excellent insulation, while thermally improved aluminum minimizes heat transfer.

Discover the charm and functionality of double hung windows. Versatile and easy to maintain, our double hung vinyl windows are a top choice for homeowners and contractors.

Benefits of Double Hung Windows

Double hung windows allow you to open both the upper and lower sashes, promoting air circulation and making them easy to clean. Their sleek, classic look complements any home style.

Double hung windows let you enjoy fresh air and promote the natural cooling of your home. They can reduce the need for artificial air conditioning, help you save on your energy bills, and foster a healthier home environment.

The innovative tilt-in design makes cleaning a breeze. Easily access both the interior and exterior surfaces from inside your home, ensuring your windows remain spotless and clear without the need for ladders or professional cleaners.

Choose from a variety of frame colors, grids, and glass types to match your home’s aesthetic, whether you want the timeless beauty of double hung windows or the modern appeal of double hung vinyl windows.

Embrace the classic beauty and modern utility of single hung windows. Cost-effective and versatile, this timeless window style is the perfect choice for nearly any project.

Benefits of Single Hung Windows

Single hung window parts include a fixed upper sash with a bottom sash that slides up and down. They’re one of the most common types of windows because of their many benefits.

Single hung windows open vertically, without protruding, allowing you to enjoy fresh air without compromising on interior or exterior space usage. They’re perfect for rooms facing patios, walkways, and porches.

These windows come in a wide variety of materials, colors, and styles. Our single hung windows are built with vinyl, aluminum, or fiberglass and come in multiple interior and exterior colors to match any design style and budget.

Modern single hung windows offer excellent insulation and are available with energy-efficient features like Low-E coatings. More and more homeowners are choosing energy-efficient single hung window replacements.

Why Choose Sliding Windows

Whether you're embarking on a new construction project or replacing old windows, our sliding glass windows are designed to elevate your space and improve your home’s energy efficiency.

Horizontal slider windows have large glass panels that glide smoothly along a track, brightening up your living spaces without compromising on privacy. They’re ideal for spaces where you want fresh air and unobstructed views.

With a range of styles and finishes, horizontal windows complement any home design, from traditional to contemporary, enhancing your home’s curb appeal. They’re also an excellent choice to satisfy bedroom egress requirements.

Upgrade to our energy-efficient windows to reduce your heating and cooling costs. Our horizontal designs provide superior insulation, keeping your home comfortable year-round and reducing your energy bills.

fieldData.main-information:
Expand your space with an elegant nook. Marvin bay and bow windows are a combination of three or more windows that angle out beyond the home’s exterior, creating an elegant interior cove for reading, chatting, or relaxing.

What is a bay window?

Bay windows most often feature one large center window with two narrow windows installed on either side. They project beyond the exterior wall of your home at an angle, creating a three-dimensional view.

What is a bow window?

Bow windows project beyond the outside of the structure and are usually composed of four or more identically sized windows that are configured in a curve instead of an angle.
